moduleDescription:
This module defines compile-time reflection procs for working with types.

Unstable API.

entries.name: HoleyEnum
entries.code: HoleyEnum = (not Ordinal) and enum
entries.description: Enum with holes.

entries.name: OrdinalEnum
entries.code: OrdinalEnum = Ordinal and enum
entries.description: Enum without holes.

entries.name: name
entries.code:
proc name(t: typedesc): string {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [], tags: [],
                                 forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the name of t.

Alias for system.`$`(t) since Nim v0.20.

entries.name: arity
entries.code:
proc arity(t: typedesc): int {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [], tags: [],
                               forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the arity of t. This is the number of "type" components or the number of generic parameters a given type t has.

entries.name: genericHead
entries.code:
proc genericHead(t: typedesc): typedesc {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Accepts an instantiated generic type and returns its uninstantiated form. A compile-time error will be produced if the supplied type is not generic.

See also:

  • stripGenericParams proc

entries.name: stripGenericParams
entries.code:
proc stripGenericParams(t: typedesc): typedesc {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: This trait is similar to genericHead, but instead of producing an error for non-generic types, it will just return them unmodified.

entries.name: supportsCopyMem
entries.code:
proc supportsCopyMem(t: typedesc): bool {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns true if t is safe to use for copyMem.

Other languages name a type like these blob.

entries.name: hasDefaultValue
entries.code:
proc hasDefaultValue(t: typedesc): bool {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if t has a valid default value.

entries.name: isNamedTuple
entries.code:
proc isNamedTuple(T: typedesc): bool {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [], tags: [],
                                       forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true for named tuples, false for any other type.

entries.name: pointerBase
entries.code: template pointerBase[T](_: typedesc[ptr T | ref T]): typedesc
entries.description: Returns T for ref T | ptr T.

entries.name: rangeBase
entries.code:
proc rangeBase(T: typedesc[range]): typedesc {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the base type for range types, or the type itself otherwise.

See also:

  • rangeBase template

entries.name: rangeBase
entries.code: template rangeBase[T: range](a: T): untyped
entries.description: Overload of rangeBase for values.

entries.name: distinctBase
entries.code:
proc distinctBase(T: typedesc; recursive: static bool = true): typedesc {.
    magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the base type for distinct types, or the type itself otherwise. If recursive is false, only the immediate distinct base will be returned.

See also:

  • distinctBase template

entries.name: distinctBase
entries.code: template distinctBase[T](a: T; recursive: static bool = true): untyped
entries.description: Overload of distinctBase for values.

entries.name: tupleLen
entries.code:
proc tupleLen(T: typedesc[tuple]): int {.magic: "TypeTrait", raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the number of elements of the tuple type T.

See also:

  • tupleLen template

entries.name: tupleLen
entries.code: template tupleLen(t: tuple): int
entries.description:
Returns the number of elements of the tuple t.

See also:

  • tupleLen proc

entries.name: get
entries.code: template get(T: typedesc[tuple]; i: static int): untyped
entries.description: Returns the i-th element of T.

entries.name: StaticParam
entries.code: StaticParam[value] = object
entries.description: Used to wrap a static value in genericParams.

entries.name: elementType
entries.code: template elementType(a: untyped): typedesc
entries.description: Returns the element type of a, which can be any iterable (over which you can iterate).

entries.name: enumLen
entries.code: macro enumLen(T: typedesc[enum]): int
entries.description: Returns the number of items in the enum T.

entries.name: genericParams
entries.code: template genericParams(T: typedesc): untyped
entries.description:
Returns the tuple of generic parameters for the generic type T.

Note: For the builtin array type, the index generic parameter will always become a range type after it's bound to a variable.

entries.name: hasClosure
entries.code: proc hasClosure(fn: NimNode): bool {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if the func/proc/etc fn has closure. fn has to be a resolved symbol of kind nnkSym. This implies that the macro that calls this proc should accept typed arguments and not untyped arguments.

entries.name: toUnsigned
entries.code: template toUnsigned(T: typedesc[SomeInteger and not range]): untyped
entries.description: Returns an unsigned type with same bit size as T.

entries.name: toSigned
entries.code: template toSigned(T: typedesc[SomeInteger and not range]): untyped
entries.description: Returns a signed type with same bit size as T.
